avoid_months: mid-May (snow too high for comfortable pass-crossing)
snow_or_rain_notes: June: high snow depth from Balu Ka Ghera onward, snow on both sides of pass
July: snow melting rapidly, good patches on higher reaches
August: most snow gone, only patches near Hampta Pass
Monsoon showers begin in July; rains last until mid-September
temperature_notes: Early October: night temperatures fall below zero at most camps, icy cold at higher camps

terrain_challenges: Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
River crossings (gushing streams, Rani Nala up to waist-deep)
Bouldery moraine sections and multiple ledges/ridges near Hampta Pass
Slushy and slippery forest trails in heavy rain
Snow-covered trail sections even in June
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek reaches 14,065 ft; AMS risk above 10,000 ft
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Fitness does not eliminate risk if ascent is too fast
Prolonged rainfall or snowfall can change trek dynamics
June snow on Balu Ka Ghera trail makes it slippery

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria

experience_themes: June: high snow from Balu Ka Ghera onward, snow on both sides of pass, snow patches on flats near Shea Goru
July: snow starts to melt rapidly, wildflowers begin (distorts, marigolds, blue poppies), first monsoon showers, green belt on Kullu side
August: most snow gone except patches near Hampta Pass, vibrant green grasslands, swaying wildflowers, cloudy skies, good showers
September: no snow on trail, post-mid-September skies clear, early autumn colours, colourful slopes, gorgeous sunrises/sunsets, snow-clad mountains against deep blue skies
June: snow-bound valley above Balu Ka Ghera, narrow valley with limited sun keeps snow depth high
July-August: vibrant green grasslands, slopes alive with swaying wildflowers, cloudy skies
primary_watchouts: Mid-May: snow too high for comfortable pass-crossing
Monsoon: landslides, roadblocks, traffic jams/delays en route to base camp
October: streams dry up, water sources increasingly difficult, early winter chill, night temps below zero
October (less fun, water scarcity, early winter chill)
Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
River crossings (gushing streams, Rani Nala up to waist-deep)
best_for: Wildflower and monsoon trekkers (mid-July to August)
not_ideal_for: Mid-May (snow too high for comfortable pass-crossing)
October (less fun, water scarcity, early winter chill)
